_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize AK state-legislature batch (5): Stedman, Giessel, Wielechowski, Foster, Frier. Alaska's bipartisan coalitions produce heavy cross-party deviations vs. the party-default archetypes. Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; never inferred from party.

bert-stedman.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (state tier). Coalition Republican (Senate Majority Coalition), Finance co-chair, fiscal pragmatist; first seated Nov 2003. DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) May 2022 voted to TABLE Sen. Myers' Amendment No. 5 barring Medicaid abortion funding (motion carried 10-8; 4 Republicans incl. Stedman joined Democrats), leaving state abortion funding intact -> sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE. (2) May 2022 voted 11-8 to TABLE SB 140 (barring transgender athletes from girls' K-12 sports), one of 5 Republicans joining Democrats to kill the biological-sex bill -> biblical_marriage q2 FALSE. Backed large PUBLIC-school funding increases (not school choice). Listed as an NRA member (affiliation only; no specific 2A vote found -> guns null, not credited). No evidence located on religious liberty, election integrity, immigration, federal-overreach votes; SB91 (2016) vote unresolved (sources conflict) -> null. Most social-cluster cells are genuinely NO EVIDENCE (the prior archetype filled them with party defaults). Running UNOPPOSED in 2026 (primary Aug 18, 2026). Religion: not documented.

1.note: May 2022: one of 4 Republicans who voted to table Amendment 5 (zero-out Medicaid abortion funding), preserving state-funded abortion -- contrary to abortion abolition.

2.note: May 2022: voted 11-8 to table SB 140 (transgender athletes barred from girls' K-12 sports), one of 5 Republicans helping kill the biological-sex bill.

cathy-giessel.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (state tier). Senate Majority Leader of the bipartisan coalition; lost a 2020 GOP primary then returned in 2022. The prior 97/100 archetype reflects her PRE-2020 reputation, not her current record. DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) Pro-choice governing position -- 'I do not support the government denying the right of Alaskans to make their own medical decisions, including to abortion, even though I am personally pro-life' (2022); sponsored SB 147/HB 195 chemical-abortion-expansion (pharmacist prescribing); Alaska Right to Life 'Life Review 33' rates her effectively pro-abortion -> sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE. (2) Sponsored SB 90 letting youth counselors meet minors on gender identity/sexual orientation WITHOUT parental knowledge/consent -> biblical_marriage q2 FALSE and family_child_sovereignty q1 FALSE (strips parental consent). (3) Guns: own FAQ supports red-flag laws, 'universal background checks are a basic requirement,' ghost-gun regulation, and restricting AR-15s to age 25+ -> self_defense q1 FALSE (old NRA 'A' rating predates these stated positions; not relied upon). Leading champion of ranked-choice voting/open primaries -- prior archetype 'voted for voter ID/hand counts' claims were unsourced and reset to null. No evidence on same-sex-marriage vote, school choice, religious liberty, crime, federal overreach, immigration -> null. Running 2026; primary Aug 18, 2026. Religion/denomination: not documented.

1.note: Pro-choice governing position; sponsored chemical-abortion-expansion bill (SB 147/HB 195); Alaska Right to Life rates her effectively pro-abortion -- contrary to abortion abolition.

2.note: Sponsored SB 90 enabling youth counselors to facilitate a minor's gender-identity/social transition without parental consent -- contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

1.note: SB 90 (her bill) removes parental notification/consent for minors' gender-related counseling -- directly contrary to parental-consent position.
Own campaign FAQ supports red-flag laws, universal background checks, ghost-gun regulation, and AR-15 age-25 restriction -- contrary to opposing red-flag laws/registries.

bill-wielechowski.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (state tier). Longtime progressive Democrat (Senate since 2007), IBEW counsel, Seton Hall (Catholic) J.D. Mixed record with genuine cross-party catches. CONSERVATIVE-DIRECTION (credit where earned): (1) Cosponsored HB 186, the 2010 Alaska Firearms Freedom Act, declaring certain Alaska firearms exempt from federal regulation (signed July 2010) -- a 10th-Amendment nullification ON guns -> refuse_federal_overreach q0 TRUE and self_defense q2 TRUE. (2) On the 2019 SB91 repeal (HB 49) he amended the bill to make first-time drug possession punishable by up to a year in jail -- a proportional-punishment toughening -> public_justice q2 TRUE. PROGRESSIVE-DIRECTION: pro-choice (stated he would have voted against SB 89 restricting abortion providers; argued against constitutional abortion-ban amendment) -> sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE; backed mail-ballot expansion (prepaid postage, ballot curing) and tribal IDs as valid voter ID -> election_integrity q1 FALSE and q3 FALSE. No evidence on marriage vote, religious liberty, immigration specifics -> null. Religion: not documented. Note: confirm 2026 filing via AK Division of Elections before treating as final ballot status.

1.note: Stated he would have voted against SB 89 (barring abortion providers in schools); has argued against constitutional amendments removing abortion rights -- pro-choice, contrary to abolition.
Backed election-access overhaul accepting tribal IDs as valid voter ID -- broader-ID/access posture contrary to photo-ID-with-citizenship-verification tightening.

3.note: Worked on mail-ballot expansion (prepaid postage, ballot curing) -- supports mass mail-in voting, contrary to opposing it.

2.note: Cross-party catch: amended HB 49 (2019 SB91 repeal) to restore up to one year jail for first-time drug possession -- toughened proportional punishment.

0.note: Cross-party catch: cosponsored HB 186 (2010 Alaska Firearms Freedom Act), a 10th-Amendment/anti-commandeering measure declaring Alaska firearms exempt from federal regulation.

2.note: Cross-party catch: HB 186 cosponsorship seeks to nullify federal gun regulation (GCA/NFA reach) over Alaska-made firearms -- aligns with opposing federal gun regulations.

neal-foster.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (state tier). Rural Inupiat Democrat (Nome/HD 39), House Finance co-chair, in the House since 2009; bipartisan House Majority Coalition. ACU AK 9% (2021), 32% lifetime; iVoterGuide 'Leans Liberal.' CONSERVATIVE-DIRECTION (credit where earned): (1) Voted YES on the 2017 SB91 rollbacks (toughening the 2016 soft-on-crime reform) -> public_justice q1 TRUE. (2) Supported HB 61 (2023) barring government from restricting firearms during declared emergencies (NRA-backed; House passed 26-12 with bipartisan support; Foster not among the named no-votes) -> self_defense q4 TRUE (anti-disarmament). PROGRESSIVE-DIRECTION: April 2023 voted to TABLE Rep. Eastman's amendment barring state Medicaid abortion funding (tabling carried 23-17) -> sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE; cosponsored HB 99 LGBTQ anti-discrimination bill -> biblical_marriage q4 FALSE. As Finance co-chair sided with R minority for a full statutory PFD (2026) but paired with an oil-tax-hike rationale (not a clean conservative marker -> null). No evidence on election integrity, religious liberty, immigration, federal overreach -> null. Religion: not documented. Note: confirm 2026 filing.

1.note: April 2023: voted to table Rep. Eastman's HB 39 amendment barring state Medicaid abortion funding (motion carried 23-17), preserving state-funded abortion -- contrary to abolition.

4.note: Cosponsored HB 99, an LGBTQ anti-discrimination bill extending protections in government services/public accommodations -- documented support for LGBTQ policy expansion.

1.note: Cross-party catch: voted yes on 2017 rollbacks of SB91, the criminal-justice-reform law widely blamed for crime increases -- tougher-on-crime direction.

4.note: Cross-party catch: supported HB 61 (2023) prohibiting government from restricting firearms/ammo or closing gun businesses during declared emergencies -- anti citizen-disarmament; House passed 26-12, Foster not among named no-votes.

robyn-niayuq-frier.notes_set: Roster-verified 2026-06-17 (state tier). Robyn Niayuq Frier is the same person as Robyn Niayuq Burke -- legally changed her name to Frier on Oct 15, 2025 (akleg member code bur->frb). First-term Inupiaq Democrat, HD 40 (North Slope/Northwest Arctic; Utqiagvik). ELECTED (not appointed) in 2024, defeating incumbent Rep. Thomas Baker via ranked-choice general (Round 2, 60.5%); sworn in Jan 21, 2025. Member of the bipartisan House Majority Coalition; co-chair House Resources. THIN RECORD -- the only HARD, self-stated positions are: increase public-school BSA + Inupiaq-language instruction; pro-resource-development (North Slope oil, Red Dog Mine); subsistence-hunting rights; public-safety investment for remote villages; signed the U.S. Term Limits (Article V) pledge. None map cleanly to the conservative rubric cells without inference, and her individual 2025 roll calls were not retrievable. Per protocol, NO social-issue positions fabricated -- all cells reset to null and confidence set roster_verified (NOT evidence) so she leaves the archetype queue honestly. Six of ten RESOLUTE areas (life, marriage, religious liberty, border, guns, most of election integrity) returned NO EVIDENCE. Note: subsistence-hunting language must NOT be read as a 2A position. Religion: not documented. Campaign site robynfrierak.com live; presumed running 2026 (primary Aug 18, 2026).
